conditions: Rightmost 4 ft piling is sharp with texture. The 11 ft piling is visible but hazy, and the 14 ft/30 ft pilings are only faint vertical shadows without clear texture. This corresponds to conditions where the 14 ft piling is a hazy silhouette and farther pilings are barely discernible.

Closest 4 ft piling at right edge is sharp with clear texture. The 11 ft and 14 ft pilings are not distinctly visible; instead there is a broad hazy piling silhouette farther back consistent with the ~14 ft distance but without clear texture. No clear view of 30 ft pilings or bottom. This matches the guideline for a hazy/faded 14 ft piling silhouette.

Right 4ft piling is sharp with clear texture. Mid/background pilings around 14–30ft are present only as hazy silhouettes without visible detail, similar to or slightly better than the 25ft reference but without bottom visible. This corresponds best to the 25ft category.

Only the nearest piling on the right edge is clearly visible with good texture; no mid or far pilings are discernible, and the background fades into uniform water column. This corresponds to visibility limited to the closest structure.

Closest 4 ft piling at right edge is sharp with clear texture. The 11 ft piling is visible but somewhat soft, and farther 14–30 ft pilings are not distinctly separated or textured, more merged into background haze. This matches conditions where only the nearest piling is clearly defined beyond short range.

Right-edge closest piling (~4 ft) is sharp with visible structure; farther pilings (11–30 ft markers) are not clearly discernible, mostly off-frame or lost in haze, so reliable range is limited to the near piling only.

Closest 4 ft piling at right edge is clear with texture. The mid-right (~11 ft) piling is visible but hazy, and the 14 ft/30 ft pilings are only barely discernible as broad shadows in the background. No clear view of distant pilings or bottom, so visibility is between 10 and 15 ft; closer to 10 ft since background is very muted.
